abstract: "Green hydrogen," produced without greenhouse gas emissions, is a cornerstone of the global decarbonization efforts mandated by the Paris Agreement. While proton exchange membrane water electrolyzers (PEMWEs) are poised to play a central role in this transition, their current market presence remains marginal, representing less than one gigawatt of global capacity. To meet climate targets, PEMWE capacity must scale dramatically to hundreds of gigawatts by 2030. This paper provides a comprehensive roadmap for the cell-level advancements required to achieve this massive deployment, incorporating industrial perspectives from three leading hydrogen technology firms. Our analysis covers the critical drivers of growth, including economies of scale, renewable energy pricing, and legislative frameworks, while also addressing technical challenges through accelerated stress testing and component-specific engineering breakthroughs.
